text: threat actors used emotet malware exploiting cve-2023-23397 to establish persistence using powershell scripts.
security analysts identified ransomware encryption behavior across several endpoints in the network.
threat actors exploited cve-2022-30190 and deployed trickbot for credential theft.
an unauthorized script attempted to connect to suspicious-host.io from ip 8.8.8.8.
the malware attempted lateral movement using smb shares and persistence via scheduled tasks.
wannacry ransomware activity was detected after exploitation of cve-2017-0144.
a malicious domain bad-actor.net was associated with phishing emails targeting employees.
threat intelligence feeds reported emotet infrastructure communicating with ip 45.33.32.1.
the attacker exploited cve-2021-44228 to execute remote code on vulnerable servers.
ransomware operators deployed payloads after compromising administrator credentials.
network monitoring detected unusual dns requests to suspicious-update.com.
a ransomware campaign leveraged trickbot and credential dumping techniques to move laterally across the network.
credential dumping utilities were executed alongside trickbot malware activity.
a remote access trojan communicated with attacker-controlled.org using encrypted traffic.
cve-2023-3519 exploitation attempts were observed against internet-facing systems.
security teams identified phishing emails but no malware execution occurred.
the infected endpoint contacted malicious-download.net from ip 203.0.113.25.
emotet and ransomware payloads were distributed after exploitation of cve-2023-23397 and communication with control-panel.com.
the attacker contacted malicious-domain.com from ip 192.168.1.5 while deploying emotet using cve-2023-23397.
a phishing campaign delivered wannacry malware through a malicious attachment exploiting cve-2021-34527.
suspicious outbound traffic was detected from ip 10.0.0.45 communicating with update-server.net.
the soc observed powershell execution and persistence mechanisms associated with trickbot malware.
an attacker exploited cve-2020-1472 to gain administrative privileges within the domain controller.
multiple failed login attempts originated from ip 172.16.5.12 targeting the finance department systems.
emotet malware established communication with command-control.org after initial compromise.
attackers exploited cve-2024-1111 and deployed ransomware across critical infrastructure servers.
